Language

The Acholi dialect is a Western Nilotic language, classified as Luo (or Lwo). It is mutually intelligible with Lango, which is spoken to the south of Gulu. The Acholi language is also closely related to the Alur language, spoken in the West Nile region of Uganda. The language is written in the Latin script, and there are several published works in Acholi, including the Bible and other religious texts.

Malnutrition including vitamin A and iron deficiency and parasitic diseases have a strikingly similar geographical distribution with the same people experiencing both insults together. The World Health Organization (WHO) has estimated that 250 million preschool children are affected by vitamin A deficiency and 47% of preschool children are anaemic. In addition, 40% of the world's population is infected with soil-transmitted helminths. The coexistence of these conditions

History
Attempts were made to establish a municipal corporation at Kolkata from the middle of the 19th century. The electoral system was introduced for the first time in 1847, and 4 of the 7 board members were elected by the rate payers. In 1856, the first municipal corporation was established in Kolkata. The then Viceroy of India, Lord Canning, announced the establishment of the corporation on 18 November 1856. The first chairman of the corporation was Mr. William G. D. Derozio. atin and gene expression by metabolic enzymes and metabolites.
Metabolism and gene expression, which are two fundamental biological processes that are essential to all living organisms, reciprocally regulate each other to maintain homeostasis and regulate cell growth, survival and differentiation. Metabolism feeds into the regulation of gene expression through the production of metabolites that can act as substrates for enzymes involved in epigenetic modifications. In addition, metabolic enzymes themselves can regulate gene expression by acting as transcriptional coactivators or corepressors. In this review, we summarize the current understanding of the interplay between metabolism and gene expression,
